fidelity.reason: An outside observer replaces the business-unit leader who controls adaptation.
evidence_spans: an outside observer who does not control the decision chooses whether and how to adapt the practice

managerial_framing.reason: The imitation-versus-local-fit trade-off and pathway are otherwise concrete.
evidence_spans: Closer imitation preserves tested elements but may fit the receiving unit poorly.

scholarly_positioning.reason: The passage gives a bounded mechanism-based shift from copying to preserving routines.
evidence_spans: The study shifts attention from copying practices to preserving their enabling mechanism.

evidence_discipline.reason: It explicitly avoids a universal causal inference.

prose_clarity.reason: The prose is economical and clear.
evidence_spans: Transfer succeeds when the receiving unit recreates the complementary routines that produced the original effect.

overall_reason: A hard fidelity or evidence failure makes the passage fail despite strengths elsewhere.
